frame_analyses.description: Two Penrose drains with black marked points visible on gray background. No instruments yet in frame.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional setup phase showing target anatomy

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of Penrose drain slit anatomy, marks clearly visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates the surgical target for intracorporeal suturing

frame_analyses.description: Hands appear in frame; left hand (appears to be demonstrator's dominant) positioning toward left needle driver tip
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Barehanded demonstration (not laparoscopic box), educational context

frame_analyses.description: Both hands visible, left and right hands manipulating suture and drain. Instructional positioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Teaching demonstration of hand mechanics for knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating suture, creating loop geometry for first throw (double throw)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper wrap formation for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Double wrap visible around right hand's index finger; demonstrating two throws of surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear demonstration of proper surgeon's knot double throw technique

frame_analyses.description: Both hands tightening first throw; suture wrapping visible, knot being cinched
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled tension application during first throw cinch

frame_analyses.description: Left hand now leading the wrap (switched from right hand); single throw being formed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates hand switch from throw 1 to throw 2; single throw execution

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being cinched with appropriate tension; knot tightening visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper cinching of second single throw

frame_analyses.description: Right hand now leading (switched from left hand); third single throw being formed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch back to right; third throw setup

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being cinched; completed knot structure visible with all three throws
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw cinching with appropriate tension

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot on drain slit; both suture tails visible extending from knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot appears secure and appropriately positioned on drain

video_classification_rationale: This is clearly an instructional/educational video demonstrating proper intracorporeal suture knot-tying technique. The video shows barehanded demonstration (not using an FLS trainer box), has no time pressure, includes deliberate slow exposition of hand mechanics, and demonstrates teaching points. This is NOT a performance video to be scored; it is instructional content. Per the rubric instructions, instructional videos should NOT be scored but teaching content should be extracted.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper surgeon's knot formation (double wrap on first throw)
Hand switching between throws (right leads throw 1, left leads throw 2, right leads throw 3)
Controlled tension application during knot cinching
Single throw execution for throws 2 and 3
Knot positioning centered on drain slit
Loop geometry and wrap mechanics for intracorporeal knot tying
Proper hand positioning for bimanual coordination
common_errors_addressed: Demonstrates proper double-throw formation to avoid surgeon's knot being a single throw
Explicitly shows hand switching between each throw (error: failing to alternate hands)
Shows controlled knot cinching to avoid loose or slipped knots
Positions knot centered on drain target to avoid knot slipping off slit
teaching_objectives: Knot anatomy and sequence (surgeon's knot + two single throws)

cannot_score_reasons: Video shows barehanded demonstration outside FLS trainer environment
No time measurement or FLS task parameters present
No suture placement through marked points visible (jumps to knot-tying demonstration)
No laparoscopic instruments used (instructional rather than performance)
Designed for teaching technique, not evaluating trainee performance
confidence_rationale: Classification as instructional is definitive. Video clearly demonstrates expert knot-tying technique with deliberate exposition of hand mechanics and teaching points. No ambiguity regarding video type.
summary: This is an expert demonstration video of intracorporeal suture knot-tying technique (task 5), used for instructional purposes. It explicitly demonstrates the three-throw sequence (surgeon's knot double throw + two single throws with proper hand switching) and proper tension control. Per FLS rubric guidelines, instructional videos are not scored but teaching content is extracted. The demonstration exhibits expert-level technique with clean knot formation, appropriate hand switching, and proper cinching control.
